title: set!-mutated variable captured by MULTIPLE closures is not shared — each closure gets a private copy
goal: Surfaced by the SICP-completeness gate (ESH-0005, ch3_accounts). A variable that is (a) mutated by set! and (b) captured by two or more closures is NOT shared: each closure captures a private copy, so one closure's set! is invisible to the others. This breaks SICP §3.1.1 make-account (message-passing object with sibling withdraw/deposit closures over a shared `balance`) and the entire 'local state / objects' style. Single-closure set!-capture works (make-withdraw, make-accumulator) and the named-let loop case was fixed in PR #50 — this is the uncovered GENERAL case.
minimal_repro: (define (make-counter n) (cons (lambda () (set! n (+ n 1)) n) (lambda () n))) (define c (make-counter 0)) ((car c)) ((car c)) (display ((cdr c)))  ;; prints 0, MUST print 2
root_cause: Closure capture copies the captured value into each closure's environment (by value). For a set!-mutated captured variable this is wrong: all closures (and the enclosing frame) must reference ONE shared mutable cell. Standard fix = assignment conversion: a variable that is set!-mutated AND captured must be heap-boxed (allocated as a single cell), with all reads/writes (in the defining frame and every capturing closure) going through that cell. PR #50 did this narrowly for named-let loop captures (arena-move set!-mutated captures + pointer-pass); generalize it to ALL set!-mutated captured variables.
next_actions: ICC-scope codegenLambda + the free-variable capture path + findFreeVariables + set! codegen. Identify where captured variables are materialized into the closure environment by value.

Detect variables that are BOTH set!-mutated AND captured by a closure (a mutated-captured set). Box them: allocate a single heap/arena cell at the defining binding; the defining frame and every capturing closure reference the same cell; set! writes through the cell; reads load through it.

Reuse/generalize the #50 mechanism (arena-move set!-mutated captures + gated pointer-pass) rather than a parallel path. Ensure it covers: internal-define sibling closures (make-account), explicit sibling lambdas (make-counter repro), nested closures, and lambdas escaping their defining frame.
NOTE: the ESH-0070 AD agent is concurrently editing llvm_codegen.cpp — keep changes localized to the closure-capture/set! region to minimize merge conflict; coordinate at integration.
acceptance: make-counter repro prints 2; SICP ch3_accounts.esk make-account cases all PASS (account-deposit=90 etc.)
set!-mutated var shared across 2+ sibling closures, nested closures, and escaping closures — all observe one cell; -r AND AOT
no regression: single-closure set! (make-withdraw/accumulator), named-let loop captures (#50 tests), closure edge tests still pass
campaign_payoff: Unblocks SICP ch3 (objects/local-state/message-passing) — a release-gate-critical swath — and fixes a fundamental closure-correctness hole. Found by the SICP-completeness gate within minutes of standing it up.
